hook.text: Every coastal nation claims 200 nautical miles of ocean as its Exclusive Economic Zone.

buildup.text: Within this zone, a country controls fishing, drilling, and mining rights.

discovery.text: When zones overlap, nations negotiate—or fight. The South China Sea is a hotspot.

twist.text: China claims almost the entire South China Sea using a 'nine-dash line' with no legal basis.

climax.text: Ocean borders are the new frontier of territorial conflict in the 21st century.

punchline.text: The next border wars won't be on land. They'll be at sea.

quiz.question: How far does an Exclusive Economic Zone extend from a coast?
